questions.question: A customer says "I don't trust online sellers — I'm worried I'll get a fake product." Which retention reason applies, and what are the key trust-building points in the script?

options.text: Reason 13 (Doubts About Quality) — emphasise quality control and 1-year warranty
Reason 10 (Lack of Trust) — highlight strong customer base, secure payment options, easy returns within 10 days, and warranty support
Reason 9 (Poor Service) — apologise and offer to make things right
Reason 12 (Negative Reviews) — ask which review they read and address their concern

questions.question: A customer says "The price was ₹1,999 when I ordered but now it shows ₹2,499 — I feel cheated." Which retention reason applies?

options.text: Reason 18 (Discount/Coupon Issue) — the customer expected a lower price
Reason 6 (Financial Issues) — the customer cannot afford the new price
Reason 11 (Price Increased / Price Changed Before Delivery) — explain pricing factors and highlight exclusive offers and loyalty points
Reason 2 (Found a Better Deal) — the customer found a lower price elsewhere

questions.question: A customer says "I read terrible reviews about this watch after placing my order." In the Reason 12 retention script, what action should the executive take that is unique to this reason?

options.text: Offer a discount code to compensate for the customer's concern
Immediately escalate to CRM Asst Manager for a review management response
Ask the customer to share more details about the review they read, and explain that Sylvi resolves all customer issues
Inform the customer that all negative reviews are from non-genuine customers

questions.question: A customer says "I entered the wrong delivery address." The order status is "Confirmed." What is the correct action per the Reason 14 retention script?

options.text: Inform the customer that address changes are not possible once an order is placed
Cancel the order and ask the customer to reorder with the correct address
Update the address in FIMS immediately and follow the address verification SOP from the "Order Confirmation Call Script — Template Library"
Escalate to CRM Asst Manager — address changes require manager approval

questions.question: A customer says "My mobile number on the order is wrong — I won't get the OTP for delivery." What does the Reason 15 script advise?

options.text: Cancel and reorder with the correct number — contact details cannot be changed
Escalate to logistics partner; the customer can collect by showing valid identity proof
Update the mobile number in FIMS and confirm with the customer
Ask the customer to contact the courier directly to update their number

questions.question: A customer says "My payment failed during checkout but the order still shows as placed." Which retention reason applies, and what is unique about this reason?

options.text: Reason 18 (Discount/Coupon Issue) — guide the customer to apply the correct code
Reason 6 (Financial Issues) — offer EMI options for the payment
Reason 17 (Payment Issues) — no retention attempt is required; advise checking with bank and placing a new order if desired
Reason 17 (Payment Issues) — attempt retention by offering alternative payment methods

questions.question: A customer says "My coupon code didn't apply and I paid full price." What does the Reason 18 script state about retroactive discounts?

options.text: The executive can manually apply the discount and adjust the order total
Escalate to CRM Asst Manager to authorise the retroactive discount
Once an order is placed, additional discounts or offers cannot be applied; reference loyalty programs for future purchases
Cancel the order and ask the customer to reorder with the coupon code

questions.question: A customer asks: "Can I change the model/colour of my order instead of cancelling?" What is the approved policy answer?

options.text: Yes — modifications are possible before shipping; update the order in FIMS
No modifications can be made once an order is placed; the customer can return or replace within 10 days of delivery (except Prototype products)
Escalate to CRM Asst Manager to check if the warehouse can swap the product
Cancel and reorder — this is the standard modification procedure

questions.question: A customer asks: "I entered the wrong address — can you update it?" What is the approved policy answer?

options.text: Address changes are never possible once an order is placed
Yes, if the order has not been shipped; once dispatched, the address cannot be changed
Only CRM Asst Manager can authorise address changes
Address changes require the customer to email support@sylvi.in with proof of address

questions.question: A customer asks: "My delivery is delayed — when will I get it?" What is the approved timeline communicated in the general policy answers?

options.text: Delivery within 3–5 business days after dispatch
Delivery within 5–7 business days after dispatch
Delivery within 7–10 business days after dispatch
No specific timeline — "we will get back to you"

questions.question: A customer asks: "Can a discount be applied now? I found a code after placing the order." What is the approved answer?

options.text: Yes — provide the code and the executive will apply it in FIMS
Escalate to CRM Asst Manager for retroactive discount approval
No — discounts cannot be applied retroactively; the loyalty program benefits apply to future purchases
Cancel the order and reorder with the discount code applied

questions.question: A customer asks: "What happens to my prepaid payment if I cancel?" What is the approved answer?

options.text: Refund within 3–5 business days as store credit
Refund within 7–10 business days to the original payment method
Refund within 24 hours via UPI reversal
Refund within 15 business days via bank transfer

questions.question: A customer claims they were never given the option to cancel before their order was shipped. What is the correct escalation?

options.text: Inform the customer that cancellation is not possible for shipped orders — no escalation needed
Escalate to CRM Asst Manager via `customer-care` Slack before dismissing the claim
Escalate to CRM Manager via `after-sale-services` Slack immediately
Cancel the shipped order as an exception — the customer's claim takes priority

questions.question: A customer wants to cancel a prepaid order worth ₹4,500 and the order status is "Shipped." What must the executive do before informing the customer that cancellation is not possible?

options.text: Proceed with the standard shipped-order script — order value does not affect the process
Escalate to CRM Asst Manager via `customer-care` Slack before informing cancellation is not possible — order is ₹3,000+ and shipped
Escalate to CRM Manager via direct call — high-value orders require manager approval
Process the cancellation as an exception — high-value prepaid orders can be cancelled even after shipping

questions.question: A customer becomes verbally abusive and threatening during a cancellation call. What is the correct escalation action?

options.text: Warn the customer, attempt to de-escalate, and continue the call
End the call immediately and log the incident in the Cancellation Order sheet
Escalate to CRM Asst Manager via direct call immediately — do not continue the call alone
Escalate to CRM Asst Manager via `customer-care` Slack and wait for instructions before ending the call

questions.question: A customer follows up because their prepaid refund has not been processed. How many business days must have passed before this becomes an escalation trigger, and what is the channel?

options.text: 5 business days → escalate via `customer-care` Slack
7 business days → escalate via `after-sale-services` Slack
10 business days → escalate to CRM Asst Manager via `after-sale-services` Slack at the customer's first follow-up
15 business days → escalate to CRM Manager via direct call

questions.question: Which escalation trigger uses the `after-sale-services` Slack channel instead of `customer-care`?

options.text: Customer claims they were never given the option to cancel before shipping
High-value shipped order (₹3,000+) cancellation request
Abusive or threatening customer on a cancellation call
Prepaid refund not processed after 10 business days

questions.question: Which of the following is listed as a common mistake in the SOP?

options.text: Not offering EMI options to every customer regardless of their cancellation reason
Using generic retention language that does not address the customer's specific concern
Attempting retention on Reason 17 (Payment Issues) calls
Not escalating every cancellation to CRM Asst Manager before processing

questions.question: The SOP lists 6 common mistakes. Which of the following correctly identifies THREE of them?

options.text: Cancelling without retention attempt; not asking the cancellation reason; not informing prepaid customers of refund status
Using the wrong Slack channel for escalation; not uploading QC images; not verifying pincode serviceability
Not sharing the RMA number; leaving the warranty field blank; not uploading the customer video
Skipping address verification for retained orders; not checking the Cancellation Order sheet format; not logging the call type

questions.question: An executive receives a cancellation call and immediately cancels the order in FIMS because the customer sounded firm. Which TWO common mistakes did they commit?

options.text: Not asking the cancellation reason AND cancelling without a retention attempt
Not updating the Cancellation Order sheet AND not informing refund status
Using generic retention language AND accepting a shipped-order cancellation
Not asking the cancellation reason AND not escalating to CRM Asst Manager
